title: Image Component
description: Display images from the web, local files, or static resources.

slides.title: Displaying Images 🖼️
slides.content:
# `<Image>`

The Image component is used to display different types of images, including network images, static resources, temporary local images, and images from local disk (like camera roll).

## Basic usage

```jsx
<Image
  source={{ uri: 'https://reactnative.dev/img/logo-og.png' }}
  style={{ width: 200, height: 200 }}
/>
```

**Always provide a width and height** for remote images, otherwise they won't appear.

slides.title: Local Images 📂
slides.content:
## Static Images

To include an image from your project's assets, use `require`:

```jsx
<Image
  source={require('./assets/icon.png')}
  style={{ width: 50, height: 50 }}
/>
```

* The path is relative to the file where you use it.
* React Native automatically bundles the image.
* You **don't** need to specify dimensions – they are inferred from the image file (but you can override with style).
* For local images, the image name must be known statically (i.e., you can't use a variable with `require`).

quizData.question: How do you include a local image named 'logo.png' located in the 'assets' folder?
quizData.explanation: For local images, you must use `require()` with the relative path.

slides.title: Resize Modes 🔄
slides.content:
## Controlling Image Fit

The `resizeMode` prop determines how the image scales when its frame doesn't match the aspect ratio.

* `'cover'` (default) – scale uniformly, maintaining aspect ratio, so that both dimensions are greater or equal to the view.
* `'contain'` – scale uniformly, maintaining aspect ratio, so that both dimensions are less than or equal to the view.
* `'stretch'` – stretch to fill the view, possibly distorting.
* `'repeat'` – repeat the image to cover the view (iOS only).
* `'center'` – center the image without scaling.

Example:
```jsx
<Image
  source={{ uri: '...' }}
  style={{ width: 100, height: 100 }}
  resizeMode="contain"
/>
```

slides.title: Image with Placeholder
slides.content: You can use `defaultSource` to show a placeholder while the remote image loads:

slides.title: Important ⚠️
slides.content: When using network images, always include dimensions. Also, be mindful of caching – React Native does not cache images by default. You may need a library like `expo-image` or `react-native-fast-image` for advanced caching.
